jokes.text: I'm about to get down and funky with this Ladybot, and I'd appreciate it if as many people as possible could know about it. Now can we get some privacy?!
jokes.setup: Bender claims he wants privacy for his romantic encounter
jokes.punchline: But first demands that as many people as possible know about it
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific paradox that captures Bender's attention-seeking narcissism while supposedly wanting privacy

jokes.text: Aw, yeah! Back it on up.
jokes.setup: Bender's romantic encounter continues
jokes.punchline: His crude commentary on the situation
jokes.explanation: Standard Bender crudeness, gets a mild laugh but nothing special

jokes.text: ♪ Hambone, hambone! ♪
jokes.setup: Romantic encounter taking a strange turn
jokes.punchline: Bender breaks into old-timey hambone dancing/singing
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd interruption that's both unexpected and perfectly Bender-esque

jokes.text: Okay, hambone break's over. Back to the bedroom!
jokes.setup: The hambone dancing was apparently just an intermission
jokes.punchline: Treating it like a formal break in structured entertainment
jokes.explanation: Great follow-up that escalates the absurdity by treating the hambone as a scheduled intermission

jokes.text: She left a while ago. She said some idiot was taking her to lunch for her birthday.
jokes.setup: Fry asking where Leela is
jokes.punchline: The Professor unknowingly calling Fry an idiot to his face
jokes.explanation: Classic dramatic irony where the audience knows what the Professor doesn't

jokes.text: ♪ What day is today? ♪ ♪ It's Leela's birthday ♪ Please stop. ♪ What a day for a birthday ♪ I really would like you to stop singing. ♪ Let's all have some cake! ♪
jokes.setup: Amy trying to cheer up stood-up Leela with birthday song
jokes.punchline: Continuing to sing despite increasingly direct requests to stop
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy that builds through escalation of polite refusals being ignored

jokes.text: You want to buy a slice of birthday cake for $11.95? Yes, please.
jokes.setup: The birthday song leads to cake offer
jokes.punchline: Absurdly expensive single slice and Leela's desperate acceptance
jokes.explanation: Solid absurd pricing gag with good deadpan delivery from both characters

jokes.text: I was. But then I realized it's actually my fault for thinking you might be on time for once.
jokes.setup: Fry asking if Leela is mad about him being late
jokes.punchline: Leela's resigned acceptance that expecting punctuality from Fry was her mistake
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific line that shows their relationship dynamic and Leela's weary acceptance

jokes.text: Wow, that'll be the nicest place I've ever been stood up.
jokes.setup: Fry promising dinner at fancy restaurant
jokes.punchline: Leela's matter-of-fact assumption she'll be stood up again
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy that's both sad and funny, with excellent economy of language

jokes.text: I can throw up on a stripper anytime. Tonight, I want to not throw up... On you.
jokes.setup: Fry explaining why he's choosing dinner with Leela over the bachelor party
jokes.punchline: The pause making it sound romantic before the crude clarification
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with the pause creating false romantic expectation before crude reality

jokes.text: Cubert, fetch my drinking teeth.
jokes.setup: Professor agreeing to be Bender's wingman
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to having special teeth for drinking
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and economical line that perfectly captures the Professor's bizarre inventiveness

jokes.text: How do you spell 'XO'? Guh!
jokes.setup: Fry trying to sign birthday card
jokes.punchline: Doesn't know how to spell two letters
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry stupidity - economically delivered character moment

jokes.text: Does it have to be nude?
jokes.setup: Hermes explains nude video greeting
jokes.punchline: Fry's innocent question about requirements
jokes.explanation: Standard straight-man reaction - functional but not remarkable

jokes.text: You leave a nude video greeting. Does it have to be nude? I guess not. That never occurred to me.
jokes.setup: Explanation of how the video greeting card works
jokes.punchline: Bender never considered that nudity might be optional
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's default assumption that everything involves nudity

jokes.text: I wouldn't want to do that again.
jokes.setup: Professor warning about not sleeping with your own grandmother
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual admission he's already done it
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that's delivered with perfect casualness, referencing established show mythology

jokes.text: Everyone we ever knew died thousands of years ago. Everyone we ever knew? Ah, I never liked those guys.
jokes.setup: The devastating realization that everyone they know is dead
jokes.punchline: Bender's callous dismissal of everyone they ever knew
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment that provides dark comedy relief from the existential horror

jokes.text: Ah, I never liked those guys.
jokes.setup: Everyone they knew is dead
jokes.punchline: Bender's callous dismissal of universal death
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response - completely heartless and perfectly in character

jokes.text: They blew it up! And then the apes blew up their society, too! How could this happen?! And then the birds took over and ruined their society! And the cows, and then... I don't know. Is that a slug maybe?
jokes.setup: Planet of the Apes reference with nuclear destruction
jokes.punchline: Escalating chain of different species taking over and ruining society, ending in confusion
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity that parodies sci-fi tropes while getting increasingly ridiculous

jokes.text: Hey, aren't you the loser who got stood up at my other restaurant?
jokes.setup: Robot recognizing Leela
jokes.punchline: Bluntly reminds her of previous embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - robot's tactless recognition adds insult to injury

jokes.text: Has anyone seen Fry's ass? It's late for a date with my boot.
jokes.setup: Looking for Fry who's late
jokes.punchline: Hermes' creative threat about kicking Fry's ass being 'late for a date' with his boot
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that fits Hermes' character but not particularly original

jokes.text: Everywhere I looked, there were piles of bodies. And then the explosion struck.
jokes.setup: Bender describing the tragic disaster
jokes.punchline: The illogical sequence - seeing bodies before the explosion that caused them
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy that subtly points out the illogical timeline in disaster reporting

jokes.text: Fry stood me up and died?! I'm so angry! I mean, I'm so sad! But-but I'm still pretty angry! But also sad. Can I be both?
jokes.setup: Leela learning Fry apparently died at the party
jokes.punchline: Her confusion about whether she can be both angry and sad simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Great character moment that captures realistic emotional confusion in a funny way

jokes.text: It's what he'd want.
jokes.setup: Leela asking if she can be both angry and sad
jokes.punchline: Amy's matter-of-fact approval
jokes.explanation: Good supportive beat - Amy's practical emotional advice

jokes.text: Man, the future is a total craphole, and whoever lives here is a crap-faced sack of crap! No offense, fellas.
jokes.setup: Fry insulting the future and its inhabitants
jokes.punchline: Politely saying 'no offense' to the people he just called 'crap-faced sacks of crap'
jokes.explanation: Standard politeness-after-insult gag, functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Man, the future is a total craphole, and whoever lives here is a crap-faced sack of crap! No offense, fellas.
jokes.setup: Bender insulting the future
jokes.punchline: Politely apologizes to future inhabitants after insulting them
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment - hilariously inappropriate politeness after crude insults

jokes.text: ♪ In the year 105105 ♪ ♪ If man is still alive ♪ ♪ If robot can survive ♪ ♪ They may find... ♪
jokes.setup: Parody of 'In the Year 2525' song
jokes.punchline: Adapting it for their time travel journey with robot inclusion
jokes.explanation: Decent parody but relies heavily on recognition of the original song

jokes.text: ♪ In the year 105105, If man is still alive, If robot can survive, They may find... ♪
jokes.setup: Time traveling through centuries
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'In the Year 2525' song
jokes.explanation: Good musical parody reference - updates classic song for sci-fi context

jokes.text: ♪ In all the world, there's only one technology ♪ ♪ A rusty sword for practicing proctology ♪
jokes.setup: Describing the limited technology of a future era
jokes.punchline: The horrifying combination of rusty sword and proctology
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully dark and absurd image that's both gross and funny

jokes.text: ♪ A shlubby merman's gonna try to get chummy ♪ ♪ He may look like a watery wimp ♪ ♪ When in fact he's a bloodthirsty shrimp! ♪
jokes.setup: Describing a seemingly harmless merman
jokes.punchline: The reveal that he's actually a bloodthirsty shrimp
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with nice rhyming structure, though the payoff is somewhat random

jokes.text: ♪ In the year one million and a half ♪ ♪ Humankind is enslaved by giraffe ♪
jokes.setup: Another future dystopia
jokes.punchline: Giraffes as the unlikely oppressor species
jokes.explanation: Solid absurd choice of oppressor, though the concept is getting repetitive

jokes.text: I laid you off 20 years ago. Woop-woop-woop-woo... Oh, the claws can't flee like they used to.
jokes.setup: Future Leela confronting Zoidberg about being laid off
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's signature panic but with aged claws that can't escape properly
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that adds the aging element to a classic Zoidberg bit

jokes.text: Oh, the claws can't flee like they used to.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg trying to escape
jokes.punchline: Age has slowed his trademark fleeing ability
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - aging affects his most iconic trait

jokes.text: And the Dumblocks... stupid, vicious brutes who live underground.
jokes.setup: Explaining human evolutionary divergence
jokes.punchline: The name 'Dumblocks' for the less evolved humans
jokes.explanation: Simple portmanteau joke that's mildly amusing but not particularly clever

jokes.text: We could settle down on that mountain of skulls.
jokes.setup: Bender wanting to stay in machine future
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic about macabre real estate
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - Bender's enthusiastic appreciation of death mountain

jokes.text: Hey! That place had a gorgeous view of Blood Lake!
jokes.setup: Discussing where they could have settled in the machine future
jokes.punchline: Bender finding the apocalyptic scenery appealing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's warped aesthetic preferences

jokes.text: Even very old and stupid males are prized.
jokes.setup: Explaining male rarity in their society
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment to the protagonists
jokes.explanation: Good insult humor - diplomatic way of calling them old and stupid

jokes.text: Let us anoint our guests in oil without using our hands. Oh, so we can stay in the future you like, but not the future I like?
jokes.setup: Amazon women offering sensual oil anointing
jokes.punchline: Bender's petty complaint about fairness while missing the obvious appeal
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Bender's contrarian nature even in obviously appealing situations

jokes.text: I could eat. And fertilize.
jokes.setup: Fertility banquet invitation
jokes.punchline: Fry understands both meanings
jokes.explanation: Simple double entendre - Fry gets the sexual implication

jokes.text: Oh, so we can stay in the future you like, but not the future I like?
jokes.setup: Being dragged away from amazon society
jokes.punchline: Bender's complaint about unfair future selection
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Bender's jealousy about future preferences

jokes.text: Somewhere, some time, Leela is waiting for me.
jokes.setup: Fry's romantic declaration stopping the fight
jokes.punchline: The pause as everyone realizes the weight of his statement
jokes.explanation: More touching than funny, but the reaction beat provides gentle humor through the dramatic shift

jokes.text: Sorry I'm a billion years late.
jokes.setup: Fry finally making it to the restaurant ruins
jokes.punchline: Casually apologizing for being a billion years late as if it were a normal tardiness
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor that treats cosmic-scale lateness as everyday rudeness, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Move it, boy toy. Granny's taking you to Atlantic City.
jokes.setup: Future Amy as an old woman with a much younger boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Her crude dominance over her 'boy toy'
jokes.explanation: Standard role-reversal comedy that's mildly amusing but predictable

jokes.text: I don't know why you ever married that pig-nosed punk.
jokes.setup: Elderly Amy dealing with divorce
jokes.punchline: Grandmother's blunt assessment of ex-husband
jokes.explanation: Standard elderly relative humor - blunt criticism of family member's choices

jokes.text: My whole life I've been mad at him, and it wasn't his fault.
jokes.setup: Hearing Fry's birthday card message
jokes.punchline: Realizes her anger was misdirected
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment - genuine emotional realization with dramatic irony

jokes.text: To the end of the universe! That's basically what I do every day.
jokes.setup: Toasting the end of the universe as a grand moment
jokes.punchline: Bender's matter-of-fact comparison to his daily routine
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender deadpan that reduces cosmic grandeur to mundane terms

jokes.text: To the end of the universe!
jokes.setup: Deciding to watch universe end
jokes.punchline: Toast to universal destruction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - treating universal apocalypse as celebratory occasion

jokes.text: Who knows. Probably some hogwash about the human spirit.
jokes.setup: Fry asking about the purpose of life
jokes.punchline: Professor's cynical dismissal of meaning as 'hogwash'
jokes.explanation: Good existential humor that fits the Professor's scientific cynicism

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah, I can take a hint.
jokes.setup: Witnessing the vast emptiness of the dying universe
jokes.punchline: Bender treating the end of existence as a social slight
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy that reframes cosmic horror as personal rejection, perfectly Bender

jokes.text: Bye, last proton.
jokes.setup: Final particle in universe decaying
jokes.punchline: Casual farewell to last bit of matter
jokes.explanation: Beautiful understated moment - treating ultimate entropy with casual friendliness

jokes.text: It's okay. They're not going anywhere.
jokes.setup: Missing dinosaurs while getting beer
jokes.punchline: Confident they'll still be there
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony - Fry's confidence about dinosaur permanence

jokes.text: It was coming right at us! You saw it. I'm gonna grab another beer.
jokes.setup: The momentous first fish crawling onto land
jokes.punchline: Fry treating it like a threat and using it to justify getting another beer
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of the evolutionary milestone moment with Fry's typical self-serving interpretation

jokes.text: Betrachten sie meinen schnurrbart!
jokes.setup: Time travelers going back through history
jokes.punchline: Hitler saying 'Look at my mustache!' in German as they pass
jokes.explanation: Mildly absurd historical moment but relies on Hitler reference without much added wit

jokes.text: Darn! I hit Eleanor Roosevelt by mistake.
jokes.setup: Fry trying to shoot Hitler from the time machine
jokes.punchline: Accidentally hitting Eleanor Roosevelt instead
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy through historical accident, though the setup is somewhat contrived

jokes.text: This new universe is about ten feet lower than our old one.
jokes.setup: Discussing the differences in the new universe
jokes.punchline: The mundane observation about height difference between identical universes
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd observation that treats cosmic differences as minor construction variations

jokes.text: That was the old Fry. He's dead now.
jokes.setup: Fry assuring Leela he'll be on time now
jokes.punchline: Literally referring to his alternate universe self as dead
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy that's technically true while sounding like typical self-improvement talk

jokes.text: That's okay. I don't really like cards. What I'll remember is our time together.
jokes.setup: Fry apologizing for losing her birthday card
jokes.punchline: Leela's sweet response that ironically references their actual time together spanning universal cycles
jokes.explanation: Sweet ending with subtle irony since their 'time together' was literally through multiple universes
